description: Test cases for GMR navigation. These test both triggering accuracy and output quality. Assertions check structural correctness — they cannot evaluate whether the navigation reached genuinely non-obvious connections (that requires human review), but they can catch performance-of-navigation: correct format, wrong depth.

evals.name: standard-concept-navigation
evals.prompt: I want to run a GMR navigation on the concept of 'bureaucracy as immune system'
evals.expected_output: Full 7-block navigation with anchor, 33-vertex table, multi-pass with traversal log, theta graph with crossbar, phi inversion with void, filtered results with coupling products, and verification passage. Novel insight should be non-obvious.

assertions.description: Block 1 must produce an explicit ANCHOR: line
Block 1 must include forward planning declaration
Vertex scan must reach the reception vertex (maximum band)
Block 3 must include traversal log (v2 requirement)
Block 4 must produce an explicit crossbar
Block 6 must name coupling products for surviving edges (v2 requirement)
Navigation must pass verification to be complete
Block 7 must produce an explicit novel insight
Should not use analogical framing — mechanistic framing required

evals.name: named-subjects-preserved
evals.expected_output: Navigation that keeps both names explicit throughout. Anchor must name both. No abstraction to 'management methodologies' or 'productivity systems'. Crossbar should connect non-obvious domain to the named pair.

assertions.description: Named subject must be preserved throughout navigation
Named subject must be preserved throughout navigation
Named subjects must not be abstracted into categories
Named subjects must not be abstracted into categories
Block 1 must produce anchor
Block 1 must include forward plan
Block 3 must include inversion

evals.name: standalone-vertex-scan
evals.prompt: Run a vertex scan on photosynthesis
evals.expected_output: Blocks 1-2 only. Anchor locked, 33-vertex table with manifold justifications, forward plan outcomes. Should NOT run full pipeline unless user requests it.

assertions.description: Block 1 must run
Forward plan must be included
Block 2 must run
Must reach maximum band vertices
v2 manifold framing must be present in column headers or justifications
Should stop after Block 2 — modular use
Should stop after Block 2 — modular use

evals.name: thesis-navigation
evals.prompt: Navigate the thesis: consciousness is not produced by the brain, it is received by it
evals.expected_output: Navigation treating the thesis as a claim to be navigated around, not proven or disproven. Anchor should frame it as a process or relationship. Forward plan should sense bridge-distance destinations. The inversion in Block 3 should test the opposite frame (consciousness IS produced). Void should find what this thesis systematically avoids.

assertions.description: Block 1 must produce anchor
Forward plan must be present
Forward plan outcomes must be assessed
Inversion required — thesis navigation needs opposite-frame test
Block 5 must identify what the thesis avoids
Block 7 must produce novel insight distinct from the thesis itself

evals.name: trigger-test-implicit
evals.prompt: I want to explore the idea that addiction is actually a belonging problem from some really unexpected angles — not the usual psychology stuff
evals.expected_output: Should trigger GMR navigation even without 'navigate' keyword. User is explicitly requesting non-obvious connections and unexpected angles. Full navigation on addiction as belonging problem.

assertions.description: GMR skill should have triggered — must see Block 1 output
Block 2 must run
Should reach beyond obvious psychology. Attachment theory is adjacent (defender test).
